WP-RESP-001.title: Complete Issue #8 Submitted Evidence Intake Path
WP-RESP-001.current_state: Draft evidence handling is guarded, but real submitted-mode packet path remains blocked until real Issue #8 evidence exists and PR #141 or successor bridge work is reconciled.
WP-RESP-001.target_state: A locally validated submitted-mode packet path exists for real Issue #8 attachments while pilot execution remains blocked.

measurable_current_state.statement: Draft evidence handling is guarded, but real submitted-mode packet path remains blocked until real Issue #8 evidence exists and PR #141 or successor bridge work is reconciled.
measurable_current_state.measurement: Measured by submitted-mode validators, Issue #8 blockers, STATUS markers, and PR #141 state.

measurable_target_state.statement: A locally validated submitted-mode packet path exists for real Issue #8 attachments while pilot execution remains blocked.
measurable_target_state.measurement: Accepted only when schema, fixtures, validator coverage, CI, docs, and boundary markers pass for submitted-mode packet handling.

target_capability.statement: Submitted-mode Issue #8 packet path is deterministic and locally checkable.
target_capability.measurement: Schema or fixture plus validator and CI-visible checks cover missing, malformed, sample, and real-path packet states.

WP-RESP-001.expected_project_delta: Repository gains schema, fixture, validator, CI, and documentation support for a submitted-mode Issue #8 packet path without creating submitted evidence or changing Issue #8 state.
allowed_work: Harden submitted-mode packet schema, fixtures, validator behavior, and CI-visible coverage.
Document how real Issue #8 evidence must be supplied without automation mutating the issue.
forbidden_work: Do not create submitted evidence unless explicitly authorized by the repository owner and current Issue #8 submitted-mode contracts.
Do not mutate Issue #8 or mark Issue #8 evidence as satisfied.
Do not run or authorize real pilot execution.
Do not claim production_ready, release_ready, live_render_validated, export_json_validated, accessibility_passed, pixel_perfect, or responsive_correctness_validated.
Do not treat CI success, merged PRs, catalog completion, Work Package completion, or queue completion as domain evidence.
must_deliver: Submitted-mode path contract or fixture coverage where applicable.
Deterministic validator coverage for missing, malformed, sample, and real-path packet states.
Documentation that pilot execution remains blocked until real evidence and readiness gates pass.
must_preserve: Rolling queue remains a historical reconciled archive, not the execution driver.
Controller selection must come only from planning/EV4_AUTOMATION_WORK_PACKAGE_CATALOG.json.
Quality gates, CI, review, and evidence boundaries remain mandatory.
Technical evidence claims remain false unless real evidence gates pass.
Catalog replenishment is state-driven and must not block active Work Package execution.
Catalog replenishment must respect the single-active-mutation-PR policy.
definition_of_done: The measurable target state is met by schema-backed or validator-backed repository evidence.
The selected PR slice remains reviewable and stays under this Work Package ID.
No submitted evidence, Issue #8 mutation, pilot authorization, or forbidden readiness claim is produced by the Work Package.

split_rule.principle: A Work Package may be large in objective, but each PR must remain reviewable.

allowed_pr_slices.title: schema and fixtures
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Add or harden submitted-mode packet contracts and valid/invalid fixtures.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

allowed_pr_slices.title: validators and CI
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Add deterministic validation and wire required checks into Validate.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

allowed_pr_slices.title: docs, STATUS, and integration
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Record the submitted-mode path and remaining evidence boundaries.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

blocked_if: Open PR #141 or successor bridge work modifies the same submitted-packet bridge files and is not reconciled.
Issue #8 real-submission source is absent or ambiguous.
completion_record_requirements: Record selected Work Package ID and PR slice ID in the PR body.
List files changed, validators changed, and CI commands run.
State commands not run and why.
Restate evidence/pilot/readiness/production/release/responsive-correctness boundaries.
Record whether catalog replenishment was checked and whether it was non-mutating, same-PR in-scope, or blocked by single-active-PR policy.
Record remaining risks and the next safe human action.

WP-RESP-003.title: Consolidate Validation Chain and Quality Debt
WP-RESP-003.current_state: Multiple validators, fixture guards, and review-debt records exist, but chain ownership and quality-debt closure need a coherent validation view.
WP-RESP-003.target_state: Primary Validate, validator index, fixture coverage, and review-debt rules are consolidated into one auditable repository validation chain.

measurable_current_state.statement: Multiple validators, fixture guards, and review-debt records exist, but chain ownership and quality-debt closure need a coherent validation view.
measurable_current_state.measurement: Measured by workflow, STATUS, command index, validator list, and named quality-debt records.

measurable_target_state.statement: Primary Validate, validator index, fixture coverage, and review-debt rules are consolidated into one auditable repository validation chain.
measurable_target_state.measurement: Accepted when workflow, STATUS, command index, validators, fixtures, and quality-debt records align without removing safety checks.

target_capability.statement: Validation chain governance is coherent, deterministic, and CI-visible.
target_capability.measurement: Workflow, STATUS, command index, validators, and named quality-debt records align without removing existing safety checks.

WP-RESP-003.expected_project_delta: Repository gains coherent validation-chain governance that makes validators discoverable, keeps STATUS/workflow parity machine-checked, and closes named quality debt without inventing checkpoint tasks.
allowed_work: Consolidate validator index and CI workflow parity.
Close named review-debt items with deterministic checks.
Improve diagnostics where validators already own the behavior.
forbidden_work: Do not create submitted evidence unless explicitly authorized by the repository owner and current Issue #8 submitted-mode contracts.
Do not mutate Issue #8 or mark Issue #8 evidence as satisfied.
Do not run or authorize real pilot execution.
Do not claim production_ready, release_ready, live_render_validated, export_json_validated, accessibility_passed, pixel_perfect, or responsive_correctness_validated.
Do not treat CI success, merged PRs, catalog completion, Work Package completion, or queue completion as domain evidence.

Named quality-debt closure records or deterministic validator improvements.
CI workflow alignment without removing existing checks.
must_preserve: Rolling queue remains a historical reconciled archive, not the execution driver.
Controller selection must come only from planning/EV4_AUTOMATION_WORK_PACKAGE_CATALOG.json.
Quality gates, CI, review, and evidence boundaries remain mandatory.
Technical evidence claims remain false unless real evidence gates pass.
Catalog replenishment is state-driven and must not block active Work Package execution.
Catalog replenishment must respect the single-active-mutation-PR policy.
definition_of_done: The measurable target state is met by schema-backed or validator-backed repository evidence.
The selected PR slice remains reviewable and stays under this Work Package ID.
No submitted evidence, Issue #8 mutation, pilot authorization, or forbidden readiness claim is produced by the Work Package.

WP-RESP-004.title: Prepare Shadow-Mode Pilot Readiness Without Production Claim
WP-RESP-004.current_state: Pilot readiness artifacts and dry-run guards exist, but real pilot execution remains blocked until real submitted evidence and readiness gates pass.
WP-RESP-004.target_state: A controlled shadow-mode preparation path is documented and validated while production, release, pilot, and responsive-correctness claims remain false.

measurable_current_state.statement: Pilot readiness artifacts and dry-run guards exist, but real pilot execution remains blocked until real submitted evidence and readiness gates pass.
measurable_current_state.measurement: Measured by dry-run validators, STATUS markers, stop conditions, and absence of real submitted evidence.

measurable_target_state.statement: A controlled shadow-mode preparation path is documented and validated while production, release, pilot, and responsive-correctness claims remain false.
measurable_target_state.measurement: Accepted when dry-run validators, docs, fixtures, and STATUS preserve false readiness and production claims.

target_capability.statement: Shadow-mode preparation is deterministic as a blocked readiness path.
target_capability.measurement: Dry-run validators, stop-condition documentation, and STATUS markers preserve false readiness and production claims.

WP-RESP-004.expected_project_delta: Repository gains a bounded shadow-mode preparation path with dry-run validators, explicit stop conditions, and completion records that cannot authorize pilot execution.

Quality gates, CI, review, and evidence boundaries remain mandatory.
Technical evidence claims remain false unless real evidence gates pass.
Catalog replenishment is state-driven and must not block active Work Package execution.
Catalog replenishment must respect the single-active-mutation-PR policy.

The selected PR slice remains reviewable and stays under this Work Package ID.
No submitted evidence, Issue #8 mutation, pilot authorization, or forbidden readiness claim is produced by the Work Package.

WP-RESP-005.title: Catalog Governance and State-Driven Replenishment
WP-RESP-005.current_state: Automation objective selection is transitioning from retired rolling queue history to an approved Work Package Catalog with automatic but state-driven replenishment.
WP-RESP-005.target_state: Catalog governance updates happen only when ready depth, project state, blockers, contracts, architecture, or real gaps require replenishment under the single-active-PR policy.

measurable_current_state.statement: Automation objective selection is transitioning from retired rolling queue history to an approved Work Package Catalog with automatic but state-driven replenishment.
measurable_current_state.measurement: Measured by catalog policy fields, schema, validator self-tests, STATUS, and controller prompt behavior.

measurable_target_state.statement: Catalog governance updates happen only when ready depth, project state, blockers, contracts, architecture, or real gaps require replenishment under the single-active-PR policy.
measurable_target_state.measurement: Accepted when schema, validator, docs, and STATUS reject fixed ordinal refresh and enforce single-active-PR replenishment limits.

target_capability.statement: Catalog governance remains deterministic and does not become a recurring fixed-ordinal refresh loop.
target_capability.measurement: Schema, validator, docs, and STATUS reject fixed ordinal refresh, arbitrary tasks, and catalog mutations that violate single-active-PR policy.

WP-RESP-005.expected_project_delta: Repository gains explicit state-driven catalog governance that allows automatic replenishment when real repository state requires it while preventing checkpoint-only refresh loops and arbitrary task invention.
allowed_work: Detect ready Work Package depth and report replenishment needs.
Prepare non-mutating replenishment plans while another mutation PR is active.
Update catalog metadata only when material Work Package state changes or same-PR scope allows it.
Create a reviewed catalog replenishment PR only when no active mutation PR blocks it and ready depth is below threshold.
forbidden_work: Do not create submitted evidence unless explicitly authorized by the repository owner and current Issue #8 submitted-mode contracts.
Do not mutate Issue #8 or mark Issue #8 evidence as satisfied.
Do not run or authorize real pilot execution.
Do not claim production_ready, release_ready, live_render_validated, export_json_validated, accessibility_passed, pixel_perfect, or responsive_correctness_validated.
Do not treat CI success, merged PRs, catalog completion, Work Package completion, or queue completion as domain evidence.
must_deliver: Catalog metadata update tied to named Work Package state change or ready-depth threshold.
Validator coverage proving catalog refresh cannot become a fixed fifth-task loop.
Documentation update explaining the state-driven trigger and single-active-PR rule.
must_preserve: Rolling queue remains a historical reconciled archive, not the execution driver.
Controller selection must come only from planning/EV4_AUTOMATION_WORK_PACKAGE_CATALOG.json.
Quality gates, CI, review, and evidence boundaries remain mandatory.
Technical evidence claims remain false unless real evidence gates pass.
Catalog replenishment is state-driven and must not block active Work Package execution.
Catalog replenishment must respect the single-active-mutation-PR policy.
definition_of_done: The measurable target state is met by schema-backed or validator-backed repository evidence.
The selected PR slice remains reviewable and stays under this Work Package ID.
No submitted evidence, Issue #8 mutation, pilot authorization, or forbidden readiness claim is produced by the Work Package.

split_rule.principle: A Work Package may be large in objective, but each PR must remain reviewable.

allowed_pr_slices.title: governance schema and validator
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Harden catalog governance rules when material state changes or ready depth falls below threshold.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

allowed_pr_slices.title: catalog state update
allowed_pr_slices.objective: Update Work Package state after a named material outcome or allowed replenishment trigger.
allowed_pr_slices.review_boundary: Reviewable PR slice under the same Work Package ID; no unrelated micro-objective may be introduced.

blocked_if: No material Work Package state changed.
Ready Work Package depth is at or above threshold.
The update exists only to keep a task count, refresh cadence, fixed ordinal, or checkpoint loop alive.
Another active mutation PR exists and the catalog mutation is not in-scope for that same PR.
completion_record_requirements: Record selected Work Package ID and PR slice ID in the PR body.
List files changed, validators changed, and CI commands run.
State commands not run and why.
Restate evidence/pilot/readiness/production/release/responsive-correctness boundaries.
Record whether catalog replenishment was checked and whether it was non-mutating, same-PR in-scope, or blocked by single-active-PR policy.
Record remaining risks and the next safe human action.
